messages.content:
Planning tasks associated with loading, sequencing, expediting, and dispatching typically fall under ________.

ANSWER
Unselected
Short-range plans

Unselected
Long-range plans

Unselected
Intermediate-range plans

Unselected
I DON'T KNOW YET
Short-range plans

Which of the following is the term used for intermediate-range capacity planning with a time horizon of 3 to 18 months?

ANSWER
Unselected
Aggregate planning

Unselected
Material requirement planning

Unselected
Strategic planning

Unselected
I DON'T KNOW YET
Aggregate planning

Which choice below best describes the counter-seasonal demand option?

ANSWER
Unselected
Developing a mix of products that smooths out demands

Unselected
Producing products like lawnmowers and sunglasses in the winter

Unselected
Lowering prices when demand is low

Unselected
I DON'T KNOW YET
Producing products like lawnmowers and sunglasses in the winter

In level scheduling, what is kept uniform from month to month?

ANSWER
Unselected
Product mix

Unselected
Inventory levels

Unselected
Production or workforce levels

Unselected
I DON'T KNOW YET
Production or workforce levels

Which term describes a planning strategy that uses two or more controllable variables to set a feasible production plan?

ANSWER
Unselected
Level production strategy

Unselected
Chase strategy

Unselected
Mixed strategy

Unselected
I DON'T KNOW YET
Mixed strategy

“An optimal plan for minimizing the cost of allocating capacity to meet demand over several planning periods” best describes ________.

ANSWER
Unselected
Graphical models

Unselected
The transportation method

Unselected
The management coefficients model

Unselected
I DON'T KNOW YET
The transportation method

Which of the following is not one of the four things needed for aggregate planning?

ANSWER
Unselected
A mathematical model that will minimize costs

Unselected
A logical overall unit for measuring sales and output

Unselected
A method for determining the relevant costs

Unselected
I DON'T KNOW YET
A mathematical model that will minimize costs

Which choice below best describes the counter-seasonal demand option?

ANSWER
Unselected
Developing a mix of products that smooths out demands

Unselected
Lowering prices when demand is low

Unselected
Producing products like lawnmowers and sunglasses in the winter

Unselected
I DON'T KNOW YET
Developing a mix of products that smooths out demands

The objective of aggregate planning is to meet demand while ________ over the planning period.

ANSWER
Unselected
Minimizing cost

Unselected
Maximizing service levels

Unselected
Minimizing stockouts

Unselected
I DON'T KNOW YET
Minimizing cost

Which of the following is not consistent with a pure level strategy?

ANSWER
Unselected
Using built-up inventory to meet demand requirements

Unselected
Finding alternative work for employees during low-demand periods

Unselected
Varying production levels and/or workforce to meet demand requirements

Unselected
I DON'T KNOW YET
Varying production levels and/or workforce to meet demand requirements

Which of these is not a characteristic that makes yield management attractive to organizations with perishable inventory?

ANSWER
Unselected
Demand can be segmented

Unselected
Service can be sold in advance of consumption

Unselected
Capacity is easily changed

Unselected
I DON'T KNOW YET
Capacity is easily changed

A firm uses the pure chase strategy of aggregate planning. It produced 900 units in the last period. Demand in the next period is estimated at 1,000 units, and demand over the next six periods (its aggregate planning horizon) is estimated to average 1,000 units.

In following the chase strategy, the firm would not ________ to meet the next period’s production.

ANSWER
Unselected
Hire workers

Unselected
Subcontract

Unselected
Lay off workers

Unselected
I DON'T KNOW YET
Subcontract
